text.title: Programmatic SEO Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the programmatic-seo-patterns methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Data MUST be complete (no nulls in headers), accurate, and refreshable. Stale or junky data = penalty + cannibalization.
rule.rationale: Programmatic pages with bad data rank worse than no page; data quality dominates.

rule.statement: Template structures MUST answer the intent (comparison / listing / location). Pages that don't match intent get penalty + low CTR.
rule.rationale: Search intent mismatch is the #1 programmatic-SEO failure; explicit intent-template mapping prevents it.

rule.statement: Each page MUST have ≥300 unique words + ≥2 unique data points + indexable schema.org markup. Below that = noindex.
rule.rationale: Thin pages trigger Helpful Content algorithmic penalty; noindex below threshold protects site authority.

rule.statement: Pages MUST be tiered by data depth: tier 1 (full data) indexed + sitemap; tier 2 (partial) indexed without sitemap; tier 3 (sparse) noindex.
rule.rationale: Tiered indexability prevents low-quality pages from dragging average page authority.

rule.statement: Every page MUST have a canonical + internal link graph (sibling + parent links). Orphan pages = wasted crawl budget.
rule.rationale: Internal linking is the #2 ranking factor for programmatic; deliberate graph beats spider-only crawl.
